name: Japanese kagutsuchi
shortDescription: Kagutsuchi is the Japanese kami of fire, whose birth caused the death of his mother, Izanami. He is a powerful and dangerous deity associated with both creation and destruction.
description: Kagutsuchi (also known as Homusubi) is a central figure in Japanese mythology, primarily known as the kami of fire. His birth is a pivotal event in the creation narrative, as it leads directly to the death of his mother, Izanami, while she is giving birth to him. This tragic event marks the separation of Izanagi and Izanami and the beginning of death in the world, forcing Izanagi to descend into Yomi (the underworld) in an attempt to retrieve her. Kagutsuchi's existence is therefore deeply intertwined with themes of creation, death, and the separation of the divine and mortal realms. While representing destructive power, fire is also seen as a purifying and transformative force in Shinto beliefs. Kagutsuchi is often associated with blacksmithing and metalworking, further highlighting the duality of fire as both a tool for creation and a destructive force. After Izanami's death, Izanagi, in his grief and rage, slays Kagutsuchi, leading to the creation of other deities from his blood. This act of divine violence underscores the complex and often violent nature of creation myths. Kagutsuchi's story serves as a cautionary tale about the power of the kami and the consequences of disrupting the natural order.

sources.title: Kojiki
sources.author: Ō no Yasumaro (translator: Donald L. Philippi)
